posts.title: ChatGPT Voice Mode Runs on a 13-Month-Old Model
posts.excerpt: Developer Simon Willison (citing Andrej Karpathy) revealed that ChatGPT's voice mode runs on a GPT-4o-era model with an April 2024 knowledge cutoff — while the text interface uses GPT-5.5. That's a…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Developer Simon Willison (citing Andrej Karpathy) revealed that ChatGPT's voice mode runs on a GPT-4o-era model with an April 2024 knowledge cutoff — while the text interface uses GPT-5.5. That's a 13+ month capability gap for ChatGPT Pro subscribers paying $200/month, not prominently disclosed. OpenAI hasn't officially responded. The constraint is latency: frontier models can't yet run real-time voice cheaply. The competitive comparison: Google's Gemini Live voice mode uses Gemini 3.5 Flash (its latest model).

---
*Sources: [Simon Willison's Blog](https://simonwillison.net/) · [Build Fast with AI](https://www.buildfastwithai.com/blogs/ai-news-today-may-28-2026)*

posts.title: Use IDE + Terminal Agent as a two-tool stack
posts.excerpt: The winning formula in 2026 is pairing a fast IDE assistant (Cursor for inline suggestions and rapid autocomplete) with a terminal agent (Claude Code for complex, multi-file refactoring and…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
The winning formula in 2026 is pairing a fast IDE assistant (Cursor for inline suggestions and rapid autocomplete) with a terminal agent (Claude Code for complex, multi-file refactoring and architecture-level tasks). 70% of developers surveyed by The Pragmatic Engineer now use 2–4 AI coding tools simultaneously rather than picking just one.

---
*Source: SitePoint / Fungies.io*

posts.title: Claude Code's new Agent View changes how you manage parallel sessions
posts.excerpt: A major update (Week 20, May 11–15) introduced Agent View, a single screen showing all active Claude Code sessions — what's running, what's waiting on input, and what's done. You can launch agents to…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
A major update (Week 20, May 11–15) introduced Agent View, a single screen showing all active Claude Code sessions — what's running, what's waiting on input, and what's done. You can launch agents to the background and check back only when needed. Fast mode now defaults to Opus 4.7, and rate limits were doubled.

---
*Source: Releasebot / Medium*

posts.title: Use `/goal` to keep Claude working across multi-turn tasks
posts.excerpt: Claude Code's new /goal command lets you define a completion condition and Claude will keep working across turns until it's met, reducing the need to manually re-prompt. The Rewind menu also now lets…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Claude Code's new `/goal` command lets you define a completion condition and Claude will keep working across turns until it's met, reducing the need to manually re-prompt. The `Rewind` menu also now lets you compress earlier context with "Summarize up to here" to manage long sessions.

---
*Source: Claude Code Docs*

posts.title: AI coding agents work best on clean, modular repos
posts.excerpt: Agents are strongest when work is clear, contained, and testable. Messy codebases create messy agents. Invest in good engineering hygiene first — the AI amplifies what's already there, for better or…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Agents are strongest when work is clear, contained, and testable. Messy codebases create messy agents. Invest in good engineering hygiene first — the AI amplifies what's already there, for better or worse.

---
*Source: DEV Community / NxCode*

posts.title: Build modularly so you can deprecate agent harnesses
posts.excerpt: The complex orchestration logic you write today might be made redundant by model improvements in months. Design agent workflows modularly so you can rip out old harnesses when native model…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
The complex orchestration logic you write today might be made redundant by model improvements in months. Design agent workflows modularly so you can rip out old harnesses when native model capabilities catch up, without a full rewrite.

---
*Source: MachineLearningMastery*

posts.title: Run 10–15 Claude sessions in parallel with Git worktrees
posts.excerpt: Assign each Claude Code session its own git worktree to prevent branch collisions. Developers running this setup report it as their single biggest throughput multiplier — great for tackling…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Assign each Claude Code session its own git worktree to prevent branch collisions. Developers running this setup report it as their single biggest throughput multiplier — great for tackling independent features, tests, and refactors concurrently.

---
*Source: f22labs.com*

posts.title: Context Engineering" replaces "Prompt Engineering" as the dominant discipline
posts.excerpt: Prompts are now treated as production code: versioned, tested, and systematically optimized. Tools like DSPy let you define what a system should do via declarative "Signatures" rather than how to…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Prompts are now treated as production code: versioned, tested, and systematically optimized. Tools like **DSPy** let you define *what* a system should do via declarative "Signatures" rather than *how* to prompt it; **PromptFoo** provides open-source test harnesses to benchmark prompts across models. Research-backed techniques yield 20–60% quality gains vs. ad-hoc prompting.

---
*Source: lushbinary.com / k2view.com*

posts.title: Agentic prompts need four core components: system prompt, tools, examples, and context state
posts.excerpt: Unlike one-shot prompts, agentic prompts are distributed across time and steps. An ambiguous instruction at step one doesn't fail immediately — it drifts, and by step seven, the agent is doing…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
Unlike one-shot prompts, agentic prompts are distributed across time and steps. An ambiguous instruction at step one doesn't fail immediately — it drifts, and by step seven, the agent is doing something you never intended. Structure your agent prompts around all four components to prevent drift.

---
*Source: MachineLearningMastery*

posts.title: Five agent design patterns every developer should know in 2026
posts.excerpt: ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, Multi-Agent Collaboration, Reflection, and Tool Use have emerged as the canonical patterns for building reliable agents. Reflection (the agent critiques its own output before…
posts.author: Chris Harper
posts.content:
ReAct, Plan-and-Execute, Multi-Agent Collaboration, Reflection, and Tool Use have emerged as the canonical patterns for building reliable agents. Reflection (the agent critiques its own output before finalizing) and Orchestrator/Sub-agent architectures (specialized sub-agents coordinated by a manager agent) are seeing the most production adoption.

---
*Source: n1n.ai / Medium*
